You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). You have Bash, Read, Grep tools.

STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[14]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_14.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 14: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Full-scale production-config loopback run. Every LLM call routes through
2	loopback/pending/ → operator responds → loopback/responses/. Real STORM (5
3	perspectives × 3 rounds), real analyzer, real verify, real wiki compose.
4	Zero OpenRouter cost; operator time is the constraint.
5	
6	Usage:
7	    python scripts/pg_loopback_full_scale.py <vector_id> <query>
8	
9	Example:
10	    python scripts/pg_loopback_full_scale.py PG_LB_FS_01 "What are the health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting?"
11	
12	Estimated per run: 150-300 LLM calls, 2-8 operator-hours, 0-35 min wall-clock
13	after all responses arrive (the wall-clock is dominated by operator response
14	latency, not pipeline compute).
15	"""
16	
17	import asyncio
18	import json
19	import logging
20	import os
21	import sys
22	import time
23	from pathlib import Path
24	
25	# ---- Full production caps (NOT minimal) ----
26	os.environ["PG_LOOPBACK_MODE"] = "1"
27	os.environ["PG_MAX_ITERATIONS"] = "4"
28	os.environ["PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR"] = "30"
29	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS"] = "5"
30	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_MIN_ROUNDS"] = "2"
31	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_PAGES_PER_ROUND"] = "6"
32	os.environ["PG_MAX_SOURCES_TO_ANALYZE"] = "60"
33	os.environ["PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_TO_EXTRACT"] = "400"
34	os.environ["PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_FOR_VERIFY"] = "2000"
35	os.environ["PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_FOR_SYNTHESIS"] = "1000"
36	os.environ["PG_VERIFY_BATCH_SIZE"] = "10"
37	os.environ["PG_STORM_ROUNDS_PER_PERSPECTIVE"] = "3"
38	os.environ["PG_STORM_CONCURRENCY"] = "2"  # keep pending queue manageable
39	os.environ["PG_MAX_OUTLINE_SECTIONS"] = "8"
40	os.environ["PG_MAX_SECTIONS"] = "8"
41	os.environ["PG_SECTION_WRITE_CONCURRENCY"] = "2"
42	os.environ["PG_SYNTHESIS_MAX_EXPANSION_PASSES"] = "2"
43	os.environ["PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES"] = "1440"  # 24h — operator-paced
44	os.environ["PG_BUDGET_GUARD_USD"] = "40"

46	# Sub-agent response latency is 30s-3min per call. Every internal timeout
47	# must be larger than the worst-case operator response plus pipeline retry
48	# overhead, otherwise cascading retries double the operator workload.
49	os.environ["PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_TIMEOUT"] = "7200"
50	os.environ["PG_STORM_INTERVIEW_TIMEOUT"] = "7200"
51	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"] = "7200"

53	os.environ["PG_VERIFY_PER_CALL_TIMEOUT"] = "7200"

55	os.environ["PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_TIMEOUT"] = "7200"
56	os.environ["PG_SECTION_WRITE_TIMEOUT"] = "7200"
57	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_MAX_TIME_SECONDS"] = "72000"
58	os.environ["PG_STORM_MAX_TIME_SECONDS"] = "72000"
59	os.environ["PG_MOST_TOTAL_TIMEOUT"] = "7200"
60	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_FETCH_TIMEOUT"] = "180"
61	os.environ["PG_LOOPBACK_TIMEOUT_SEC"] = "14400"
62	os.environ["PG_PLANNER_TIMEOUT"] = "7200"
63	os.environ["PG_REACT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"] = "7200"

103	    ],
104	)

107	async def main():
108	    from src.polaris_graph.graph import build_and_run
109	
110	    banner = "=" * 78
111	    print(banner, flush=True)
112	    print(f"  {VECTOR_ID}: LOOPBACK FULL-SCALE — production config, zero LLM cost", flush=True)
113	    print(f"  Query: {QUERY[:70]}", flush=True)
114	    print(f"  App:   {APPLICATION} | Region: {REGION}", flush=True)
115	    print(banner, flush=True)
116	
117	    start = time.time()
118	    try:
119	        result = await build_and_run(
120	            vector_id=VECTOR_ID,
121	            query=QUERY,
122	            application=APPLICATION,
123	            region=REGION,
124	            stage=1,
125	            max_iterations=4,
126	            max_execution_minutes=1440,
127	        )
128	        elapsed = time.time() - start
129	        qm = result.get("quality_metrics") or {}
130	        usage = result.get("llm_usage") or {}
131	
132	        print(flush=True)
133	        print(banner, flush=True)
134	        print(f"  {VECTOR_ID} COMPLETED in {elapsed:.0f}s ({elapsed/60:.1f} min)", flush=True)
135	        print(f"  status:              {result.get('status')}", flush=True)
136	        print(f"  iteration_count:     {result.get('iteration_count')}", flush=True)
137	        print(f"  converged:           {result.get('converged')}", flush=True)
138	        print(f"  quality_gate_result: {result.get('quality_gate_result')}", flush=True)
139	        print(f"  evidence:            {len(result.get('evidence', []))}", flush=True)
140	        print(f"  claims:              {len(result.get('claims', []))}", flush=True)
141	        print(f"  bibliography:        {len(result.get('bibliography', []))}", flush=True)
142	        print(f"  sections:            {len(result.get('sections', []))}", flush=True)
143	        print(f"  total_words:         {qm.get('total_words', 0)}", flush=True)
144	        print(f"  total_citations:     {qm.get('total_citations', 0)}", flush=True)
145	        print(f"  zero_cite_sections:  {qm.get('zero_cite_sections', '?')}", flush=True)
146	        print(f"  unique_sources:      {qm.get('unique_sources', 0)}", flush=True)
147	        print(f"  faithfulness_score:  {qm.get('faithfulness_score', 0)}", flush=True)
148	        print(f"  perspective_entropy: {result.get('perspective_entropy', 0)}", flush=True)
149	        print(f"  llm calls:           {usage.get('total_calls', 0)}", flush=True)
150	        print(f"  llm cost (phantom):  ${usage.get('total_cost_usd', 0)}", flush=True)
151	        print(banner, flush=True)
152	
153	        # Gate check
154	        gate_failures = []
155	        if qm.get("total_citations", 0) < 20:
156	            gate_failures.append(f"total_citations={qm.get('total_citations', 0)}<20")
157	        if qm.get("zero_cite_sections", 99) != 0:
158	            gate_failures.append(f"zero_cite_sections={qm.get('zero_cite_sections', '?')}!=0")
159	        if qm.get("faithfulness_score", 0) < 0.80:
160	            gate_failures.append(f"faithfulness={qm.get('faithfulness_score', 0)}<0.80")
161	        if qm.get("unique_sources", 0) < 5:
162	            gate_failures.append(f"unique_sources={qm.get('unique_sources', 0)}<5")
163	        if result.get("perspective_entropy", 0) < 0.55:
164	            gate_failures.append(f"perspective_entropy={result.get('perspective_entropy', 0)}<0.55")
165	        if result.get("status") != "complete":
166	            gate_failures.append(f"status={result.get('status')}!=complete")
167	
168	        if gate_failures:
169	            print(f"  GATE: FAILED — {', '.join(gate_failures)}", flush=True)
170	            sys.exit(1)
171	        else:
172	            print(f"  GATE: PASSED", flush=True)
173	    except Exception as exc:
174	        elapsed = time.time() - start
175	        print(f"\n  {VECTOR_ID} CRASHED after {elapsed:.0f}s: {exc}", flush=True)
176	        import traceback
177	        traceback.print_exc()
178	        sys.exit(2)
179	
180	
181	if __name__ == "__main__":
182	    asyncio.run(main())

2	possible LLM calls. Pipeline halts at every LLM call, prompt is written to
3	loopback/pending/, response is written to loopback/responses/ by the operator
4	(Claude Code session). Zero OpenRouter cost; external search/fetch cost ~$0.10.
5	
6	Caps overridden in-process so .env doesn't need to be touched:
7	- PG_MAX_ITERATIONS=1  (single iteration; covers core path, skips gap-search re-iter)
8	- PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR=10
9	- PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS=2
10	- PG_AGENTIC_MIN_ROUNDS=1
11	- PG_AGENTIC_PAGES_PER_ROUND=2
12	- PG_MAX_SOURCES_TO_ANALYZE=10
13	- PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_TO_EXTRACT=50
14	- PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_FOR_VERIFY=50
15	- PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_FOR_SYNTHESIS=50
16	- PG_VERIFY_BATCH_SIZE=10
17	- PG_STORM_ROUNDS_PER_PERSPECTIVE=1
18	- PG_MAX_OUTLINE_SECTIONS=4
19	- PG_SECTION_WRITE_CONCURRENCY=2
20	- PG_SYNTHESIS_MAX_EXPANSION_PASSES=0  (skip expansion to save calls)
21	
22	Estimated LLM calls: ~25-40 total. ~5-10 conversation turns to process.
23	"""
24	
25	import asyncio
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import sys
30	import time
31	from pathlib import Path
32	
33	# Force tiny scope BEFORE any imports that read env at module load time
34	os.environ["PG_LOOPBACK_MODE"] = "1"
35	os.environ["PG_MAX_ITERATIONS"] = "1"
36	os.environ["PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR"] = "10"
37	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS"] = "2"
38	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_MIN_ROUNDS"] = "1"
39	os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_PAGES_PER_ROUND"] = "2"
40	os.environ["PG_MAX_SOURCES_TO_ANALYZE"] = "10"

54	# LOOPBACK LATENCY OVERRIDES — sub-agent responses take 30-60s vs GLM ~5s.

70	os.environ["PG_REACT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"] = "3600"

1	"""Phase 1 smoke test: verify LoopbackLLMClient produces pending files and blocks.
2	
3	Spawns a background task that calls client.generate(), watches loopback/pending/
4	for the request file, writes a canned response, confirms client returns.
5	
6	Proves the loopback mechanism works end-to-end at the client layer.
7	"""
8	import asyncio
9	import json
10	import os
11	import sys
12	import time
13	from pathlib import Path
14	
15	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
16	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
17	os.chdir(ROOT)
18	
19	from dotenv import load_dotenv
20	load_dotenv(override=False)
21	
22	# Force loopback on for this test
23	os.environ["PG_LOOPBACK_MODE"] = "1"
24	
25	from src.polaris_graph.llm.loopback_client import (
26	    LoopbackLLMClient, PENDING_DIR, RESPONSES_DIR,
27	)
28	
29	
30	async def main():
31	    print("=" * 72)
32	    print("  Phase 1: LoopbackLLMClient smoke test")
33	    print("=" * 72)
34	
35	    # Clean the dirs to ensure deterministic state
36	    for d in (PENDING_DIR, RESPONSES_DIR):
37	        for f in d.glob("*.json"):
38	            f.unlink()
39	
40	    client = LoopbackLLMClient(session_id="smoke_test")
41	
42	    async def caller():
43	        """Make one generate call — should block until response appears."""
44	        t0 = time.time()
45	        response = await client.generate(
46	            prompt="Say hello in 3 words.",
47	            system="You are a minimal test responder.",
48	            max_tokens=128,
49	            timeout=30.0,
50	        )
51	        elapsed = time.time() - t0
52	        return response, elapsed
53	
54	    async def responder():
55	        """Watch pending/, write response, confirm cleanup."""
56	        # Wait for a pending file to appear (should be within ~1s)
57	        deadline = time.time() + 10
58	        req_path = None
59	        while time.time() < deadline:
60	            pending = list(PENDING_DIR.glob("req_*.json"))
61	            if pending:
62	                req_path = pending[0]
63	                break
64	            await asyncio.sleep(0.2)
65	
66	        if req_path is None:
67	            print("[FAIL] No pending file appeared within 10s")
68	            return False
69	
70	        print(f"[OK] Pending file appeared: {req_path.name}")
71	
72	        # Read request to confirm structure
73	        with open(req_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
74	            req = json.load(f)
75	        print(f"[OK] Request structure: call_type={req.get('call_type')}, "
76	              f"prompt_len={len(req.get('prompt',''))}, "
77	              f"system_len={len(req.get('system',''))}")
78	
79	        # Write response
80	        req_id = req["request_id"]
81	        resp_path = RESPONSES_DIR / f"resp_{req_id}.json"
82	        with open(resp_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
83	            json.dump({
84	                "content": "hello from smoke",
85	                "reasoning": "",
86	                "input_tokens": 10,
87	                "output_tokens": 5,
88	            }, f)
89	        print(f"[OK] Response written: {resp_path.name}")
90	        return True
91	
92	    # Run caller + responder concurrently
93	    caller_task = asyncio.create_task(caller())
94	    responder_task = asyncio.create_task(responder())
95	
96	    responded = await responder_task
97	    if not responded:
98	        caller_task.cancel()
99	        print("[FAIL] Responder did not complete")
100	        return 1
101	
102	    try:
103	        response, elapsed = await asyncio.wait_for(caller_task, timeout=5.0)
104	    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
105	        print("[FAIL] Client did not return after response was written")
106	        return 1
107	
108	    print(f"[OK] Client returned in {elapsed:.2f}s")
109	    print(f"[OK] Response content: '{response.content}'")
110	    print(f"[OK] Response tokens in/out: {response.input_tokens}/{response.output_tokens}")
111	
112	    # Assertions
113	    checks = {
114	        "pending_appeared": True,
115	        "content_propagated": response.content == "hello from smoke",
116	        "tokens_recorded": response.input_tokens == 10 and response.output_tokens == 5,
117	        "archived": not (RESPONSES_DIR / f"resp_{response.raw_response['request_id']}.json").exists(),
118	    }
119	    print()
120	    for name, ok in checks.items():
121	        print(f"  [{'PASS' if ok else 'FAIL'}] {name}")
122	
123	    passed = sum(1 for v in checks.values() if v)
124	    total = len(checks)
125	    print(f"\n  SUMMARY: {passed}/{total}")
126	    print("=" * 72)
127	    return 0 if passed == total else 1
128	
129	
130	if __name__ == "__main__":
131	    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
132	    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))
133

1	"""
2	Mesh Scale Test -- full ask() path with real LLM on 5 sources.
3	
4	Tests the complete pipeline at moderate scale:
5	  5 sources -> extract -> entities -> edges -> ask() with follow-up
6	
7	This validates:
8	  - Edge formation at scale (do corroboration edges form between 5 sources?)
9	  - Citation diversity (do answers cite 3+ unique sources?)
10	  - Thread follow-up with real coreference
11	  - Entity deduplication across sources
12	
13	Usage:
14	    python scripts/pg_mesh_scale_test.py
15	"""
16	
17	import asyncio
18	import logging
19	import re
20	import sys
21	import time
22	from pathlib import Path
23	
24	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
25	
26	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
27	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh import MeshStore
28	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.claim_extract import extract_claims_from_source
29	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.edge_discovery import discover_edges_for_claims
30	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.ingest import ingest_file
31	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.qa.ask import ask
32	
33	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING)
34	logger = logging.getLogger("scale_test")
35	logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
36	
37	SOURCES = {
38	    "gac_filtration.md": """# GAC Filtration for PFAS Removal
39	
40	Granular activated carbon (GAC) filtration is widely used for removing per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) from drinking water. In a 12-month study across 15 municipal treatment plants, GAC achieved an average removal efficiency of 85% for long-chain PFAS compounds including PFOS and PFOA, with contact times standardized at 10 minutes per treatment cycle.
41	
42	The effectiveness of GAC varies significantly by PFAS chain length. Long-chain compounds (C8 and above) showed removal rates of 85-95%, while short-chain PFAS (C4-C6) like PFBS showed substantially lower removal at 40-60%. The carbon bed lifetime before breakthrough averaged 18 months for long-chain compounds but only 6-8 months for short-chain variants.
43	
44	Cost analysis indicates GAC treatment adds approximately $0.15-0.25 per 1000 gallons of treated water. The EPA has recommended GAC as one of the best available technologies (BAT) for PFAS removal under the proposed Maximum Contaminant Levels.""",
45	
46	    "reverse_osmosis.md": """# Reverse Osmosis for PFAS Treatment
47	
48	Reverse osmosis (RO) membrane technology demonstrates superior PFAS removal performance compared to adsorption-based methods. A multi-site study involving 42 independent measurements across 8 water utilities found RO membranes achieved 95% or greater removal of all tested PFAS compounds, regardless of chain length.
49	
50	RO maintains greater than 90% removal efficiency across all chain lengths tested (C4 through C14), making it the preferred technology for mixed-chain-length contamination. However, RO generates a concentrated reject stream containing 15-25% of the input water volume. Energy consumption averages 3-5 kWh per 1000 gallons, making RO approximately 2-3 times more expensive than GAC.""",
51	
52	    "ion_exchange.md": """# Ion Exchange Resins for PFAS Treatment
53	
54	Anion exchange (AIX) resins represent a promising alternative to GAC for PFAS removal. Single-use AIX resins achieved 99% removal of both long-chain and short-chain PFAS in pilot studies at three water utilities. Unlike GAC, AIX resins maintain high performance for short-chain compounds including PFBS and PFHxS.
55	
56	The regenerable variant of AIX resins showed 85-90% removal after 500 bed volumes, with performance declining to 60-70% after 2000 bed volumes. Cost comparison with GAC shows AIX is 30-50% more expensive per treatment unit but offers superior removal of the increasingly regulated short-chain PFAS compounds.""",
57	
58	    "nanofiltration.md": """# Nanofiltration Membranes for PFAS
59	
60	Nanofiltration (NF) membranes provide an intermediate option between reverse osmosis and conventional filtration. NF membranes with molecular weight cutoff below 300 Daltons achieved 90-95% removal of long-chain PFAS, though performance dropped to 70-80% for short-chain compounds in a 6-month pilot study.
61	
62	NF requires lower operating pressures (70-120 psi) compared to RO (150-300 psi), resulting in 40% lower energy consumption. Water recovery rates for NF are typically 80-85% compared to 75-85% for RO, producing less reject stream. Treatment costs range from $0.20-0.35 per 1000 gallons.""",
63	
64	    "comparative_review.md": """# Comparative Review of PFAS Treatment Technologies
65	
66	A comprehensive meta-analysis of 47 peer-reviewed studies compared the four leading PFAS treatment technologies. GAC filtration remains the most widely deployed method, installed at over 200 US water utilities, but its limitation with short-chain PFAS is a growing concern as EPA regulations expand beyond PFOS and PFOA.
67	
68	RO and NF membrane technologies offer superior broad-spectrum removal but at higher capital and operating costs. Ion exchange resins represent the best balance of removal efficiency and selectivity for mixed PFAS contamination scenarios. No single technology is optimal for all situations -- the choice depends on the specific PFAS profile, water quality parameters, and regulatory requirements at each site.""",
69	}
70	
71	QUESTION_1 = "What are the most effective methods for removing PFAS from drinking water, and how do they compare?"
72	QUESTION_2 = "Which method works best for short-chain PFAS specifically?"
73	QUESTION_3 = "What about the costs?"
74	
75	
76	async def main():
77	    import tempfile
78	    base_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="mesh_scale_"))
79	    db_path = base_dir / "scale_test.db"
80	    logger.info("Working directory: %s", base_dir)
81	
82	    store = MeshStore.open(db_path)
83	    client = OpenRouterClient(model="z-ai/glm-5.1")
84	
85	    try:
86	        # -- Create workspace --
87	        ws_id = store.create_workspace(
88	            name="Scale Test",
89	            root_question=QUESTION_1,
90	        )
91	        logger.info("Workspace: %s", ws_id)
92	
93	        # -- Ingest 5 sources --
94	        src_ids = []
95	        for filename, content in SOURCES.items():
96	            fpath = base_dir / filename
97	            fpath.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
98	            src_id, _ = ingest_file(
99	                store=store, workspace_id=ws_id,
100	                file_path=fpath, kind="upload",
101	                url=f"https://example.com/{filename}",
102	            )
103	            src_ids.append(src_id)
104	            logger.info("Ingested: %s -> %s", filename, src_id)
105	
106	        # -- Extract claims from all 5 sources --
107	        all_claim_ids = []
108	        t0 = time.time()
109	        for src_id in src_ids:
110	            result = await extract_claims_from_source(
111	                client=client, store=store,
112	                workspace_id=ws_id, source_page_id=src_id,
113	                query=QUESTION_1,
114	            )
115	            all_claim_ids.extend(result.inserted_claim_ids)
116	            logger.info(
117	                "Extracted: %d claims (%d seen, skipped=%s)",
118	                len(result.inserted_claim_ids),
119	                result.total_facts_seen, result.skipped,
120	            )
121	        extract_time = time.time() - t0
122	
123	        # -- Discover edges --
124	        edge_result = discover_edges_for_claims(
125	            store, workspace_id=ws_id,
126	            new_claim_ids=all_claim_ids,
127	        )
128	
129	        # -- Full ask() with thread --
130	        t1 = time.time()
131	        r1 = await ask(
132	            client, store,
133	            workspace_id=ws_id,
134	            question_text=QUESTION_1,
135	        )
136	        r2 = await ask(
137	            client, store,
138	            workspace_id=ws_id,
139	            question_text=QUESTION_2,
140	            parent_question_id=r1.question_id,
141	        )
142	        r3 = await ask(
143	            client, store,
144	            workspace_id=ws_id,
145	            question_text=QUESTION_3,
146	            parent_question_id=r2.question_id,
147	        )
148	        ask_time = time.time() - t1
149	
150	        # -- Stats --
151	        stats = store.workspace_stats(ws_id)
152	        entities = store._conn.execute(
153	            "SELECT canonical_name, entity_type FROM entities "
154	            "WHERE workspace_id = ? ORDER BY canonical_name",
155	            (ws_id,),
156	        ).fetchall()
157	        quarantined = store.get_quarantined_entities(ws_id)
158	
159	        # -- Report --
160	        print("\n" + "=" * 70)
161	        print("MESH SCALE TEST REPORT")
162	        print("=" * 70)
163	
164	        print(f"\n  EXTRACTION ({extract_time:.0f}s):")
165	        print(f"    Sources: {len(src_ids)}")
166	        print(f"    Total claims: {len(all_claim_ids)}")
167	        print(f"    GOLD: {stats['gold_claims']}  "
168	              f"SILVER: {stats['silver_claims']}  "
169	              f"BRONZE: {stats['bronze_claims']}")
170	
171	        print(f"\n  ENTITIES ({len(entities)}):")
172	        for e in entities[:15]:
173	            print(f"    {e['canonical_name']} ({e['entity_type']})")
174	        if len(entities) > 15:
175	            print(f"    ... and {len(entities) - 15} more")
176	        print(f"    Quarantined: {len(quarantined)}")
177	
178	        print(f"\n  EDGES:")
179	        print(f"    Total: {edge_result.edge_ids.__len__()}")
180	        print(f"    Corroborate: {edge_result.corroboration_count}")
181	        print(f"    Contradict: {edge_result.contradiction_count}")
182	
183	        print(f"\n  Q&A THREAD ({ask_time:.0f}s):")
184	        for i, (q, r) in enumerate([
185	            (QUESTION_1, r1), (QUESTION_2, r2), (QUESTION_3, r3)
186	        ], 1):
187	            cites = re.findall(r"\[\d+\]", r.answer_text)
188	            unique = len(set(cites))
189	            words = len(r.answer_text.split())
190	            print(f"\n    Q{i}: {q[:60]}...")
191	            print(f"    Gap: {r.gap_category}  Words: {words}  "
192	                  f"Citations: {len(cites)} ({unique} unique)  "
193	                  f"Bib: {len(r.bibliography)}")
194	            print(f"    Preview: {r.answer_text[:100]}...")
195	
196	        # -- Pass/fail --
197	        total_claims = len(all_claim_ids)
198	        total_edges = len(edge_result.edge_ids)
199	        q1_unique = len(set(re.findall(r"\[\d+\]", r1.answer_text)))
200	
201	        print(f"\n{'=' * 70}")
202	        print("VERDICT:")
203	        checks = [
204	            ("Claims >= 15", total_claims >= 15),
205	            ("Entities >= 10", len(entities) >= 10),
206	            ("Edges >= 2", total_edges >= 2),
207	            ("Q1 gap = IN_SCOPE", r1.gap_category == "IN_SCOPE"),
208	            ("Q1 unique citations >= 2", q1_unique >= 2),
209	            ("No CoT leakage Q1", "<think>" not in r1.answer_text),
210	            ("Thread has 3 turns", r3.question_id != r1.question_id),
211	        ]
212	        for name, passed in checks:
213	            status = "PASS" if passed else "FAIL"
214	            print(f"  [{status}] {name}")
215	
216	        all_pass = all(p for _, p in checks)
217	        print(f"\n{'OK' if all_pass else 'WARNING'}  "
218	              f"{'ALL CHECKS PASSED' if all_pass else 'SOME CHECKS FAILED'}")
219	        return 0 if all_pass else 1
220	
221	    except Exception as exc:
222	        logger.error("Scale test failed: %s", exc, exc_info=True)
223	        return 1
224	    finally:
225	        await client.close()
226	        store.close()
227	
228	
229	if __name__ == "__main__":
230	    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

1	"""
2	Unit 2 CP-D stress test (not part of the pytest suite — a one-shot
3	validator that exercises ingest + claim_extract end to end with a
4	mock LLM and a realistic multi-source workspace).
5	
6	Run with:
7	    python scripts/pg_mesh_unit2_stress.py
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import asyncio
13	import shutil
14	import sys

67	            f"  Statement: {e['statement']}\n"
68	            f"  Quote: \"{e['quote']}\"\n"
69	            f"  Source: {e['source_title']}\n"
70	            f"  URL: {e['source_url']}\n"
71	        )
72	    return "\n".join(lines)

20	def test_filler_stripping():
21	    """Test that filler words are removed from content."""
22	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.report_assembler import _clean_filler_and_tables
23	
24	    content = (

28	        "In addition, cardiovascular risks were noted [3]. "
29	        "Indeed, the hazard ratio was 1.91 [3]. "
30	        "Consequently, clinical practice should consider these trade-offs. "
31	        "Specifically, eating windows under 8 hours showed elevated risk [3]. "
32	        "Significantly, the meta-analysis included 99 randomized trials [1]."
33	    )
34	    # Note: citations count may drop by 1 because "Additionally, " at start
35	    # removes content before the first claim, but the claim itself stays
36	    cleaned = _clean_filler_and_tables(content)
37

43	        if c > 0:

46	    citations_remaining = len(re.findall(r"\[\d+\]", cleaned))
47	
48	    print(f"  Fillers: {fillers_remaining} (was 8)")
49	    print(f"  Citations preserved: {citations_remaining} (should be 8)")
50	    print(f"  Content: {cleaned[:200]}...")
51	
52	    passed = fillers_remaining == 0 and citations_remaining >= 7

40	def format_evidence(evidence: list[dict]) -> str:
41	    lines = []
42	    for e in evidence:
43	        tier = e.get("tier", "BRONZE")
44	        verified = "[VERIFIED]" if e.get("verified") else "[UNVERIFIED]"
45	        lines.append(

48	            f"  Statement: {e['statement']}\n"
49	            f"  Quote: \"{e['quote']}\"\n"
50	            f"  Source: {e['source_title']}\n"
51	            f"  URL: {e['source_url']}\n"
52	        )
53	    return "\n".join(lines)

56	def analyze_citations(content: str, evidence: list[dict]) -> dict:
57	    """Analyze citation format, accuracy, and coverage."""
58	    cite_matches = re.findall(r"\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9_]+)\]", content)
59	    src_matches = re.findall(r"\[SRC-\d+\]", content)
60	    bracket_matches = re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", content)
61	
62	    valid_ids = {e["evidence_id"] for e in evidence}
63	    cited_ids = set(cite_matches)
64	    hallucinated = cited_ids - valid_ids
65	    coverage = len(cited_ids & valid_ids) / len(valid_ids) if valid_ids else 0
66	
67	    return {
68	        "cite_count": len(cite_matches),
69	        "src_count": len(src_matches),
70	        "bracket_count": len(bracket_matches),
71	        "unique_cited": len(cited_ids),
72	        "valid_cited": len(cited_ids & valid_ids),
73	        "hallucinated_ids": hallucinated,
74	        "coverage": coverage,
75	        "word_count": len(content.split()),
76	        "format_ok": len(cite_matches) > 0 and len(src_matches) == 0,
77	    }

1692	def _playwright_cache_roots() -> list:
1693	    """FX-16 (#1131): candidate Playwright browser-cache roots in resolution order — the official
1694	    PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH override first (skipping the special value '0' = install-next-to-package),
1695	    then the per-OS defaults (Linux/VM, Windows, macOS). Fixes the P2 false-FAIL on non-Linux installs."""
1696	    roots: list = []
1697	    env_root = os.getenv("PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH", "").strip()
1698	    if env_root and env_root != "0":
1699	        roots.append(Path(env_root))
1700	    home = Path.home()
1701	    roots.append(home / ".cache" / "ms-playwright")              # Linux / VM default
1702	    roots.append(home / "AppData" / "Local" / "ms-playwright")    # Windows default
1703	    roots.append(home / "Library" / "Caches" / "ms-playwright")   # macOS default
1704	    return roots
1705	
1706	
1707	def _find_chromium_binary(cache_root: Optional[Path] = None) -> Optional[str]:
1708	    """FX-16 (#1131): return the path to an EXISTING Playwright chromium launcher file, or None. Pure +
1709	    cross-platform (globs the Linux/Windows/macOS launcher layouts across all candidate cache roots) so
1710	    it is unit testable with a synthetic ``cache_root`` (which, when given, is the ONLY root searched).
1711	    Only a real file (``is_file()``) counts — a partial cache dir without the binary does NOT pass."""
1712	    roots = [Path(cache_root)] if cache_root is not None else _playwright_cache_roots()
1713	    patterns = (
1714	        "chromium-*/chrome-linux*/chrome",
1715	        "chromium-*/chrome-win*/chrome.exe",
1716	        "chromium-*/chrome-mac*/Chromium.app/Contents/MacOS/Chromium",
1717	    )
1718	    try:
1719	        for root in roots:
1720	            if not root.exists():
1721	                continue
1722	            for pattern in patterns:
1723	                for hit in sorted(root.glob(pattern)):
1724	                    if hit.is_file():
1725	                        return str(hit)
1726	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a probe must never crash the preflight
1727	        return None
1728	    return None

1731	async def test_chromium_browser_available() -> TestResult:
1732	    """FX-16 (#1131): fail closed when the AccessBypass browser-fetch tier (Playwright/Crawl4AI) is
1733	    dead. drb_72 fetched at success_rate 0.51 because chromium was absent on the VM and the cascade
1734	    SILENTLY fell back to httpx-naive (LAW II — no silent downgrade on a paid run). Probe the chromium
1735	    binary; if absent AND the cascade is not intentionally disabled, FAIL CLOSED (LIVE/paid path) with
1736	    remediation. DRY mode SKIPs with the same remediation so dev/CI runs do not break."""
1737	    name = "chromium_browser_available"
1738	    # Codex iter-1 P1: match the PRODUCTION opt-out EXACTLY (live_retriever.py:1764 uses
1739	    # `os.getenv("PG_DISABLE_ACCESS_BYPASS", "0") == "1"` — exact, no strip/truthy). A broader match
1740	    # here (e.g. accepting "true") would SKIP the preflight while production STILL runs the dead cascade
1741	    # → the exact silent httpx-naive fallback this gate exists to prevent.
1742	    if os.getenv("PG_DISABLE_ACCESS_BYPASS", "0") == "1":
1743	        return TestResult(name, SKIP, "PG_DISABLE_ACCESS_BYPASS=1 -- browser-fetch tier intentionally off")
1744	    binary = _find_chromium_binary()
1745	    if binary:
1746	        return TestResult(name, PASS, f"chromium present: {binary}")
1747	    remediation = (
1748	        "Playwright chromium binary not found under ~/.cache/ms-playwright -- the AccessBypass "
1749	        "browser-fetch tier is DEAD (fetch will silently degrade to httpx-naive, ~0.51 success). "
1750	        "Run 'python -m playwright install chromium --with-deps' on this host, or set "
1751	        "PG_DISABLE_ACCESS_BYPASS=1 to intentionally disable the cascade."
1752	    )
1753	    if LIVE_MODE:
1754	        return TestResult(name, FAIL, remediation)
1755	    # DRY mode: do not break dev/CI; surface the gap as an actionable SKIP that WOULD fail a paid run.
1756	    return TestResult(name, SKIP, f"[would FAIL in LIVE/paid mode] {remediation}")
